title: vulpia membranacea subsp. fasciculata
intro: Vulpia membranacea subsp. fasciculata, commonly known as fascicled hairgrass, is a plant species within the grass family (Poaceae). It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of fascicled hairgrass include the ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Fascicled hairgrass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for internal use, as well as being used topically for wound healing and skin conditions.
